mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in non-farm payroll employment indicates a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion, which typically translates into strong consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales growth above a 5% annualized rate signals strong consumer confidence and spending, directly boosting corporate revenues and profits, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%, the S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate and an accommodative federal funds rate indicates a 'sweet spot' for equities, where labor market strength supports demand without immediate inflation or monetary tightening pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If periods where UNRATE is below 6% and FEDFUNDS is below 1% consistently coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and subsequent negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods officially designated as economic recession, a rise in UNRATE by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months does not lead to negative S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 70% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, indicating robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) shows sustained annual growth above 3.5%, it signals significant inflationary pressures, which can erode corporate profit margins and prompt central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A strong level of housing starts combined with a very low federal funds rate indicates a robust housing market supported by highly accommodative monetary policy, which stimulates broad economic activity and boosts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, when HOUST exceeds 1000 thousand units and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 35% of triggered instances over any 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed contraction in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) signals a recessionary environment, leading to widespread reductions in corporate earnings forecasts and investor risk aversion, driving S&P 500 forward returns negatively.
failure_condition: If, during periods of negative quarter-over-quarter annualized GDPC1 growth, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over any 10-year period.
